name: Session Rescue
single_purpose: Session Rescue saves local snapshots of the user's open browser tabs and windows so they can restore, search, export, and import browser sessions.

permission_justifications.alarms: Runs low-frequency local snapshots only after the user enables autosave.
permission_justifications.tabs: Reads open tab URLs, titles, pinned state, and window grouping, and restores selected sessions when the user requests it.

data_categories: Web browsing activity limited to tab URLs, tab titles, window grouping, pinned state, and capture timestamps
privacy_practices.storage: All session snapshots are stored locally in IndexedDB in the user's browser profile.
privacy_practices.network: The extension does not send session data, tab URLs, searches, imports, exports, or usage events to a server.
privacy_practices.limited_use: Use of information received from Chrome APIs complies with the Chrome Web Store User Data Policy, including the Limited Use requirements.
privacy_practices.ads_or_sale: No ads, analytics, sale of user data, or third-party transfer.

reviewer_notes: No account, login, API key, backend, or paid service is required.
The extension stores tab metadata only and does not capture page content.
Autosave is off by default and starts only after the user enables it in the popup.
Use https://session-rescue.pages.dev/review/ for exact smoke-test steps and sample backup JSON content.
